sanskrit: त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यंत्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् । त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्तासनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे ॥
hindiTranslation: आप ही जाननेयोग्य परम अक्षर अक्षरब्रह्म हैं, आप ही इस सम्पूर्ण विश्वके परम आश्रय हैं, आप ही सनातनधर्मके रक्षक हैं और आप ही अविनाशी सनातन पुरुष हैं  ऐसा मैं मानता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: You are the imperishable supreme reality, the highest support of this world, the guardian of eternal dharma, and the eternal person.
essence: What appears overwhelming is actually the world’s deepest support.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna recognizes Krishna as the highest imperishable reality, the universe's support, the protector of eternal dharma, and the eternal person.
simpleInsight: Awe turns into trust when the mind sees what never changes.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still overwhelmed by Krishna's cosmic form. After seeing limitless faces and forms, he tries to name what he is beholding: the imperishable source, the world's support, and the guardian of eternal order.
modernRelevance: You stand before a decision that could reshape your life, and the usual markers fail. In that moment, the mind reaches for something unshakable to trust.
translationLiteral: त्वम् (you) / अक्षरम् (imperishable) / परमम् (supreme) / वेदितव्यम् (to be known) / त्वम् (you) / अस्य (of this) / विश्वस्य (of the universe) / परम् (highest) / निधानम् (support, resting place) / त्वम् (you) / अव्ययः (unchanging) / शाश्वत-धर्म-गोप्ता (guardian of eternal dharma) / सनातनः (eternal) / त्वम् (you) / पुरुषः (person) / मतः (considered) / मे (by me)
insight: It shifts the mind from seeing power as force to seeing it as the ground that holds all order.
